deathPlace.address: New Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America

burialPlace.address: Woodstock Hill Cemetery, Woodstock, Windham, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Edward was christened 22 January 1631/2 at Great Amwell parish in Hertfordshire, about 20 miles north of London, the son of Edward Morrison and his wife Prudence Heath.  He was the youngest known child and his father died shortly afterwards.  He may have been raised by members of his mother's Heath relatives until he was old enough to travel to America. His sister Elizabeth also went to New England as a young girl.  

He and his sister might have lived with either of two Heath uncles or an aunt in Roxbury in the Massachusetts Bay Colony.  The first record of him is when he was granted land in Roxbury on 23 February 1652/3. Roxbury was one of the first towns founded in the Massachusetts Bay Colony in 1630. 

In 1655 Edward married Grace, whose last name was spelled something like Bett.  Together they had eight known children: 
1. Isaac
2. Edward
*3. Grace
4. Ebenezer
5. Elizabeth
6. Margaret
7. Samuel
8. Martha

He was admitted to the church in 1658 and all of his children were baptized by the famous Reverend John Eliot.

In 1661 he was made chairman of a committee to survey the common land of the town, and was later paid to maintain it.  He was chosen one of the constables of the town in 1664.  Edward was chosen as a selectman of the town in 1674 and two years later as a trustee for the high school in Jamaica Plain.  In 1678 he was chosen as deputy to the General Court and served there for many years.  The town of Roxbury gradually ran out of space for an expanding population and in 1682 Edward was involved in the purchase of a large plot of land from the Nipmuck Indians in northern Connecticut. After the land was examined by a small group which included his oldest son Isaac, Edward's family was one of the 40 families who decide to settle the town of New Roxbury in 1686.  The town was later renamed Woodstock in 1690. 

As one of the elders of the new town Edward was active in laying it out and was chosen as one of the first selectmen.  In 1689 when there was rising concern over troubles with Indians Edward was named a Lieutenant, though there is no record of any military engagement. 

Woodstock didn't become part of the Connecticut Colony until 1749.

He became the first person to die in Woodstock in 1690 at the age of 57. The Woodstock Hill Burial Ground is a small cemetery established in 1686, and it is likely he was buried there.  There appears to be an older headstone of some kind there, as well as a modern marker.   

There has been much written about Edward in the old days which was well-intentioned but wrong.  Several publications from the 1800s list the incorrect parents for Edward.  For correct information about Edward and his family see the article, "The Heath Connection: English Origins of Isaac and William Heath of Roxbury, Massachusetts, John Johnson, Edward Morris, and Elizabeth (Morris) Cartwright," in The New England Historical and Genealogical Register, Vol 146, 1992: see page 277 (attached under Sources.)

sourceList.title: Edward Morris, "Massachusetts, Town Clerk, Vital and Town Records, 1626-2001"

zh.lifeSummary: 當Edward Morris於1630年8月30日出生在英國艾可沃爾坦 艾比時，他的父親Edward Morris11627歲，母親Prudence Heath32歲。他於1655年與Grace Bett在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可波斯頓結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1689年9月14日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼享年59歲，埋葬在美國康乃狄克州文達哈姆武德斯托可武德斯托可 希爾 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Edward Morris于1630年8月30日出生在英国艾可沃爾坦 艾比时，他的父亲Edward Morris11627岁，母亲Prudence Heath32岁。他于1655年与Grace Bett在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可波斯頓结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1689年9月14日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼享年59岁，埋葬在美国康涅狄格州文達哈姆武德斯托可武德斯托可 希爾 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Morris
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: from Maurice, an Old French personal name introduced to Britain by the Normans. It is derived from Latin Mauritius, a derivative of Maurus (see Moore ). This was the name of several early Christian saints. In some cases it may be a nickname of the same derivation for someone with a swarthy complexion.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Muirghis, a variant of Ó Muirgheasa (see Morrissey ).

Welsh: Anglicized form of the personal name Meurig (from Latin Mauritius), which was gradually superseded in Wales by Morus, Morys, a derivative of the Anglo-Norman French form of the name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Moore
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Mauritz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Armin, Frieda, Helmut, Kurt, Reinhold, Wolfgang.
German and Dutch: from the personal name Mauritz, from Latin Mauritius (see Morris ).

variants.name: Mauricio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Juan, Carlos, Mario, Roberto, Lilia, Luis, Adela, Andres, Antero, Cipriano.  Portuguese Manoel.
Portuguese (Maurício) and Spanish: from the personal name Maurício (Portuguese), Mauricio (Spanish), derived from the Latin name Mauritius (see Morris ).

variants.name: Maurice
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Pierre, Armand, Denys, Gabrielle, Antoine, Benoit, Camille, Cecile, Francois, Gaston, Germain.
French, English, Welsh, Scottish, and Irish: from the Old French personal name Maurice, Latin Mauritius (see Morris ).

variants.name: McMorris
meanings: Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Muiris, a patronymic from a Gaelicized form of Morris . In some cases this is a Gaelicized form of Fitzmaurice .
Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Muirgheasa ‘son of Muirgheas’, a Gaelic personal name meaning ‘sea valor’ or ‘sea choice’. Compare Morrissey .

variants.name: Morici
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Antonio, Gasper, Aldo, Angelo, Annamarie, Elvio, Giuseppe, Sal, Santo, Vito.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of a variant of the personal name Maurice (see Morris ).
Hungarian: patronymic from the personal name Móric, Hungarian form of Morris .

variants.name: Moris
meanings: English: variant of   Morris  .

French, Luxembourgish, Flemish, Walloon, North German, and Dutch: from a vernacular form of the French personal name Maurice (see Morris ). In North America, the surname Moris may also be an altered form of French Maurice .
Americanized form of German   Moritz  .

variants.name: Merrick
meanings: Welsh: from the personal name Meuric, the Middle Welsh form of Maurice (see Morris ).
Irish: borrowed from Welsh. In Ireland, when this is not the Welsh name, it might be adopted for Irish Ó Mearadhaigh, see Merry 2.

variants.name: Maurizio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carlo, Marcello, Marco, Mario, Fernando, Matteo, Natale, Sal, Valentino, Vito.
Italian: from the personal name Maurizio, from Latin Mauritius (see Morris 1).

variants.name: Morehouse
meanings: English (Yorkshire): habitational name from any of the many places called Moorhouse in Yorkshire or Durham (from Old English mōr ‘moor’ + hūs ‘house’). In some cases the surname may have become Morris . This name is also established in Ireland.

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

variants.name: Edoardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Eduardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Eideard
meanings: Gaelic equivalent of Edward .

variants.name: Édouard
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Edvard
meanings: form of English Edward .

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.
Oldest grave seen in the Memorials list.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: When Edward Morris was born on 30 August 1630, in Waltham Abbey, Essex, England, his father, Edward Morris, was 11627 and his mother, Prudence Heath, was 32. He married Grace Bett in 1655, in Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 14 September 1689, in Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 59, and was buried in Woodstock Hill Cemetery, Woodstock, Windham, Connecticut, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Edward Morris est né le 30 août 1630, à Waltham Abbey, Essex, Angleterre, son père, Edward Morris, avait 11627 ans et sa mère, Prudence Heath, avait 32 ans. Il a épousé Grace Bett en 1655, à Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 14 septembre 1689, à Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 59 ans, et a été enterré à Woodstock Hill Cemetery, Woodstock, Windham, Connecticut, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Edward Morris am 30. August 1630 in Waltham Abbey, Essex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Edward Morris 11627 und seine Mutter Prudence Heath 32. Er hat 1655 in Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America Grace Bett geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb am 14. September 1689 im Alter von 59 Jahren in Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America und wurde in Woodstock Hill Cemetery, Woodstock, Windham, Connecticut, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Morris è nato il il 30 agosto 1630 nel Waltham Abbey, Essex, England, il suo padre, Edward Morris, aveva 11627 anni e il suo madre, Prudence Heath, aveva 32 anni. Lui sposato Grace Bett nel 1655, nel Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 14 settembre 1689, nel Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 59 anni, e fu sepolto a Woodstock Hill Cemetery, Woodstock, Windham, Connecticut, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Edward Morrisが1630年8月30日にイギリスエセックスワルサム アビーで生まれた時，父Edward Morrisは11627歳，母Prudence Heathは32歳であった。1655年にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクボストンでGrace Bettと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1689年9月14日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーで59歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国コネチカット州ウィンドハムウッドストックウッドストック ヒル セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Edward Morris(이)가 1630년 8월 30일 영국 에세스 월섬 애브브이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edward Morris(은)는 11627세였고, 그의 어머니 Prudence Heath(은)는 32세였다. 그는 1655년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 보스턴에서 Grace Bett(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1689년 9월 14일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이에서 59세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 코네티컷 주 윈드함 우드스톡 우드스톡 힐 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.30 August 1630–14 September 1689: 30 de agosto de 1630–14 de setembro de 1689

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Morris nasceu em 30 de agosto de 1630, em Waltham Abbey, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Edward Morris, tinha 11627 anos e sua mãe, Prudence Heath, tinha 32 anos. Ele casou-se com Grace Bett em 1655, em Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 14 de setembro de 1689, em Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 59 anos, e foi sepultado em Woodstock Hill Cemetery, Woodstock, Windham, Conecticute, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edward Morris родился 30 августа 1630 г. в местности валтам эбби, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Edward Morris было 11627 лет, а матери, которую звали Prudence Heath, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Grace Bett в 1655 г. в местности бостон, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. Он умер 14 сентября 1689 г. в местности массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 59 лет и был похоронен в местности вудстокк хилл кеметири, вудстокк, виндхам, Коннектикут, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Edward Morris nació el 30 de agosto de 1630, en Waltham Abbey, Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, Edward Morris, tenía 11627 años y su mamá, Prudence Heath, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Grace Bett en 1655, en Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 14 de septiembre de 1689, en Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 59 años, y fue sepultado en Woodstock Hill Cemetery, Woodstock, Windham, Connecticut, Estados Unidos.
